articles.full_text:
Heatwave in Mumbai? Mumbaikars React After Scorching Afternoon as Temperatures Rise in City
By Lokmat Times Desk | Updated: March 4, 2026 14:56 IST2026-03-04T14:51:54+5:302026-03-04T14:56:32+5:30
Mumbaikars are witnessing extreme climate conditions in the first week of March itself, as the afternoon temperature on Wednesday ...
Mumbaikars are witnessing extreme climate conditions in the first week of March itself, as the afternoon temperature on Wednesday clocked 37 degrees Celsius. Streets and parks were seen as silent in the daytime and corporate workers avoided going out in the Sun.
After extreme climate conditions, netizens rush to social media sites asking whether the heatwave conditions in the city have prevailed. Several X users (formerly known as Twitter), reacted to sudden weather changes.
An X user said he felt like a "hot oven" when he went out of his office for a walk after lunch in Mumbai's Airoli area. He reported the temperature at 38°C with 16% humidity.
Did a post lunch walk in office campus at Airoli.
March 4, 2026
Temperature at 38°C with 16% humidity felt like hot oven which Mumbaikars are not used to.
Thankfully there's enough shade to walk and avoid any adverse affects of heat wave#Mumbai#Thane#Raigad#Palghar Heatwave
"Did a post lunch walk in office campus at Airoli. Temperature at 38°C with 16% humidity felt like hot oven which Mumbaikars are not used to. Thankfully there's enough shade to walk and avoid any adverse affects of heat wave," @Explorer_Yash wrote further.
Heat wave in Mumbai temperatures between 35 to 41 degrees C simply crazy .. stay safe @mybmc@CMOMaharashtra@ShelarAshish@Dev_Fadnavis#climatechange
March 4, 2026
Another user tagging BMC, Maharashtra CM Devendra Fadnavis and others said, "Heat wave in Mumbai temperatures between 35 to 41 degrees C simply crazy .. stay safe."
what’s going on!!
— Siddharth Shetty (@CmpoundMindset) March 4, 2026
heatwave in mumbai? pic.twitter.com/ZN2lYfFTDr
#Mumbai
— Parth (@Parth_GPT) March 4, 2026
It's 11:00 am & Temp' has already hit 34/5°C (real feel 38°C🌡️), over most of the areas, actual will hit 39°C by 2pm
This heatwave-like condition will stay till March 9, with temp peaking to 41°C🌡️(interior MMR)
Also, "Sweaty/Sticky" nights are expected from March 12 https://t.co/zXXiWciTm9pic.twitter.com/NR4QrPwPbC
A Mumbaikar said, "It's 11:00 am & Temp' has already hit 34/5°C (real feel 38°C🌡️), over most of the areas, actual will hit 39°C by 2 pm. This heatwave-like condition will stay till March 9, with temp peaking to 41°C🌡️(interior MMR)."
Gonna be a scorcher out there this afternoon for #NaviMumbai#Thane#KDMC etc. with 40°C+.... Just 10am and already 32°C!!
— Athreya Shetty 🇮🇳 (@shetty_athreya) March 4, 2026
First time this season temps have risen so quickly 🥵 This heatwave spell on peak next 3 days#Heatwave#MumbaiSummerhttps://t.co/WuIHs2Ig8Cpic.twitter.com/j6fv7aBWHD

A very hot day for Mumbai, Thane & Palghar today as IMD issues hot & humid condition warning at isolated places for March 4. pic.twitter.com/plHO5pHpk9
— Richa Pinto (@richapintoi) March 4, 2026
According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD) a yellow alert was issued and warned of hot and humid conditions in Mumbai and Mumbai Metropolitan Region (MMR), including Palghar, Thane, and Raigad. The weather department forecasted hot and humid conditions, most likely in isolated pockets in these districts on Wednesday (March 4) and Thursday (March 5).
Skies are expected to remain clear with daytime temperatures between 35 and 40 degrees Celsius, as the conditions will remain humid and uncomfortable.

articles.full_text:
First week of march brought the heatwave in Mumbai as the temperature in many parts reached above 30 degree Celsius. . The India Meteorological Department was said to have issued a Yellow Alert for March 4 to 6 covering the districts of Thane, Palghar and Raigad, cautioning about hot and humid conditions. Forecasters indicated that maximum temperatures could climb to 37 degrees Celsius during this period.
The IMD's March-May 2026 Hot Weather Outlook predicted above-normal minimum and maximum temperatures for most of India. An increased number of heatwave days is expected, particularly in east and east-central India, parts of the southeastern peninsula, and areas of northwest and west-central India. Specifically, above-normal heatwave days are anticipated in West Rajasthan, Gujarat, Haryana, Punjab, parts of Maharashtra, eastern Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Jharkhand, Gangetic West Bengal, Odisha, Chhattisgarh, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, north interior Karnataka, and sections of north Tamil Nadu.
Mumbai Suburbs Weather Update
